skill.name: pexoai-agent
skill.description: Produce short videos (5-120 seconds) through the Pexo AI video creation platform. Supports product ads, social media content, brand videos, and explainers with multiple aspect ratios.
skill.summary: AI-powered video creation agent that produces professional short videos through the Pexo platform, handling project management, asset uploads, and delivery.
skill.author: pexoai

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer detected 534 patterns across 13 files (2110 lines) with a risk score of 100/100. After semantic evaluation, all findings are confirmed as FALSE POSITIVES. The skill is a legitimate CLI client for the Pexo AI video platform. Shell commands (curl, jq, mktemp, stat) are standard CLI tooling. Network requests target only the configured PEXO_BASE_URL (default: pexo.ai). File access is limited to the skill's own config directory (~/.pexo/config) and temp files. Environment variable access reads only documented PEXO_API_KEY and PEXO_BASE_URL. No prompt injection, credential exfiltration, data exfiltration, or malicious behavior was detected. The skill acts as a relay between the user and Pexo's backend API.

medium_findings.title: Shell scripts execute external commands extensively
medium_findings.description: The skill uses curl, jq, mktemp, stat, basename, sed, awk, and other shell utilities across 349 locations. These are standard CLI tools used for HTTP requests, JSON processing, and file operations. All arguments are either hardcoded or derived from user-provided inputs that are validated locally before use. This is expected behavior for a shell-based API client.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Pattern confirmed through full code review. All shell commands serve legitimate CLI client purposes with no injection vectors.

medium_findings.title: Shell scripts source configuration from hidden directory
medium_findings.description: Scripts source ~/.pexo/config which sets PEXO_BASE_URL and PEXO_API_KEY. This is the documented configuration mechanism. The config file path is configurable via PEXO_CONFIG environment variable. This is standard practice for CLI tool configuration.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Confirmed legitimate config pattern. Path is documented and configurable. No evidence of unauthorized file access.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs in documentation and scripts
low_findings.description: Multiple files contain hardcoded references to https://pexo.ai and related endpoints. These are the service's own URLs, documented as the default PEXO_BASE_URL. The base URL is configurable, allowing users to override the default.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: URLs point to the service's own documented domain. PEXO_BASE_URL is configurable, mitigating lock-in risk.

low_findings.title: Temporary file creation in shell scripts
low_findings.description: Scripts create temporary files using mktemp for HTTP response bodies, headers, and error output. Files are cleaned up after use with rm -f. This is standard practice for shell-based HTTP clients.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: mktemp is the safe way to create temp files. Cleanup with rm -f is present. No temp file leakage detected.

low_findings.title: System information queries in diagnostic script
low_findings.description: pexo-doctor.sh runs command -v and --version checks to verify curl, jq, and file are installed. This is standard environment validation for a CLI tool dependency checker.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: command -v and --version are standard dependency checks. No sensitive system information is collected.

content.user_title: Create short videos with Pexo AI
content.value_statement: Creating professional videos requires expensive tools and specialized skills. Pexo Agent lets you produce short videos through AI by managing the entire workflow from project creation to final delivery.
actual_capabilities: Create and manage video projects through the Pexo API
Upload image, video, and audio files as reference assets
Submit video creation requests with user specifications
Poll for production status and handle preview selections
Deliver final videos with signed download URLs
Manage credits and handle billing-related errors
limitations: Requires a valid Pexo API key and account credits
Video production takes 15-20 minutes and cannot be accelerated
Supported file types are limited to common image, video, and audio formats
Cannot produce videos longer than 120 seconds or shorter than 5 seconds

use_cases.title: Social media content creation
use_cases.description: A social media manager creates daily TikTok and Instagram Reels content by describing the desired video and letting Pexo handle the creative production.

use_cases.title: Product marketing videos
use_cases.description: An e-commerce store owner uploads product photos and requests a promotional video, receiving a professional ad within minutes without hiring a video editor.

use_cases.title: Educational explainer videos
use_cases.description: A teacher creates short explainer videos for students by describing the topic and desired format, generating visual learning materials quickly.

prompt_templates.title: Basic video request
prompt_templates.prompt: Make a 15-second video of a cat playing in the garden, suitable for Instagram Reels.
prompt_templates.scenario: A user wants a simple short video for social media

prompt_templates.title: Product video with uploaded asset
prompt_templates.prompt: I want a 30-second product ad for my running shoes. Use this photo as the reference.
prompt_templates.scenario: A business owner uploads a product photo and requests a promotional video

prompt_templates.title: Multi-language video request
prompt_templates.prompt: フィールドで犬が遊ぶ10秒間の動画を作ってください。
prompt_templates.scenario: A Japanese-speaking user requests a short video of a dog playing in a field

prompt_templates.title: Revision request for existing project
prompt_templates.prompt: The video looks great, but can you make the background music more upbeat and add a title screen at the start?
prompt_templates.scenario: A user wants to revise a completed video with specific feedback

output_examples.input: Make a 15-second video of a sunset over the ocean, in 9:16 aspect ratio for TikTok.
output: Your video request has been submitted to Pexo.

output_examples.input: Here is the product photo. Make a 30-second ad for my running shoes.
output: File uploaded successfully. Asset ID: a_xK7mN2pQ
Your product video request has been submitted to Pexo.

best_practices: Be specific in your video descriptions. Include details about style, mood, and purpose for better results.
Use high-quality reference images when uploading assets. Clear photos produce better video outputs.
Do not send additional messages while the video is being produced. Wait for the completion notification before requesting revisions.
anti_patterns: Do not call the chat endpoint while the project is in a waiting state. This triggers duplicate video production and wastes credits.
Do not manually add creative directions that the user did not request. The AI agent specializes in video production and makes better creative decisions.
Do not share or truncate signed download URLs. The full URL including all query parameters is required for the video to load correctly.

faq.question: How long does it take to produce a video?
faq.answer: A short 15-second video typically takes 15-20 minutes. Longer or more complex videos may take additional time.

faq.question: What file formats can I upload?
faq.answer: Images: jpg, jpeg, png, webp, bmp, tiff, heic, heif. Videos: mp4, mov, avi. Audio: mp3, wav, aac, m4a, ogg, flac.

faq.question: What happens if I run out of credits?
faq.answer: When credits reach zero, video production stops. You can purchase more credits at https://pexo.ai/home by clicking Credits in the top-right corner.

faq.question: Can I revise a video after it is completed?
faq.answer: Yes. Use the same project ID and send your revision feedback through the chat. The AI agent will produce an updated version.

faq.question: What aspect ratios are supported?
faq.answer: Three aspect ratios are available: 16:9 (landscape), 9:16 (portrait for mobile), and 1:1 (square).

faq.question: Do I need to install any software to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. You need curl, jq, and the file command installed. You also need a Pexo API key configured in ~/.pexo/config. Run the diagnostic script to verify your setup.
